annotations.mention: degeneration of ovarian follicles and Sertoli and

text: Biological plausibility of this KER lies in the fact that lipid peroxidation in gonad membranes induces morphological changes in seminiferous tubules, and degeneration of ovarian follicles and Sertoli and Leydig cells in testicles, damage to gametic cells, and, consequently, reduction of their viability. This directly affects animal reproductive capability, for it reduces quality and production of oocytes and spermatocytes, as well as decreases egg and sperm release (spawn), leading to a drop-in fertilization rate (Tillitt et al. 2010; Papoulias et al. 2014; Song et al. 2014; Dasmahapatra et al. 2020; Biswas et al. 2020; Mu et al. 2022).
